area_name: Nanyang Primary School 1km balloting zone â€” King's Road / Coronation, District 10 (Bukit Timah)
summary:
Nanyang Primary (52 King's Road, 268097) is a SAP/GEP top-10 primary; balloting is routine even within 1km (Phase 2C 2025: 63 applicants/42 places; 2024: 79/40), so genuine <1km address is the price of entry. The tight 1km ring is dominated by prime freehold landed on King's Road, King's Drive/Kingsmead, Coronation Road / Coronation Road West, and Prince/Princess of Wales Road â€” terrace, semi-D and linked-bungalow character â€” plus the only HDB pocket (Farrer Gardens, 99-yr) and a handful of freehold condos (Watten House, The Asana, Duchess cluster). Formal GCB Areas (Leedon Park, Bin Tong Park, Belmont, Cornwall Gardens, Chatsworth) mostly straddle or sit just beyond the 1km line; Leedon Park's northern edge grazes it.

Land pricing: non-GCBA semi-D/terrace ~S$1,800â€“2,400 psf land (quantum ~S$5â€“20m); GCB land ~S$1,900â€“3,500+ psf (quantum ~S$28â€“60m). Recent prints include Coronation Rd West linked bungalows from ~S$15.98m and a semi-D at S$19.8m (~S$2,202 psf), and a Cornwall Gardens GCB at S$28.3m on 14,681 sqft (~S$1,928 psf, Jul 2025).

Headline watch-outs: documented April-2025 flash flooding on King's Road (Prince Rdâ€“Lutheran Rd) and Coronation Walk, with chronic ponding at the Stevens/Balmoral low point in eastern Bukit Timah; PUB Bukit Timah Canal widening completing ~2026. Foreign buyers are largely shut out of landed (LDAU approval, 5-yr no-sell, no-rent, 1,800 sqm cap; 60% ABSD), which thins the resale pool. Sophisticated buyers price land + rebuild (S$350â€“700 psf GFA), not the asking price of an ageing house.

hazards_watchouts.where: Blackmore Drive / King Albert Park, off Bukit Timah Rd

regulatory: Foreign ownership: landed residential largely barred â€” LDAU approval required (â‰¥5-yr PR + economic contribution), S$1,220 fee, ~30 working days; owner-occupier only (no renting), 1,800 sqm cap, cannot sell within 5 years.
ABSD 2026: Citizen 0%/20%/30%; PR 5%/30%/35%; Foreigner flat 60%. Foreigner on S$2m landed pays ~S$1.27m duty.
BSD 2026 progressive 1â€“6%; ~S$69.6k on S$2m, ~S$102.6k on S$2.5m; due within 14 days.
SSD (4-yr regime from 4 Jul 2025): 16/12/8/4% yr 1â€“4, 0% after yr 4.
Tenure ~73% freehold / ~14% 999-yr / ~13% 99-yr; 99-yr ~S$1â€“1.5m discount, financing tightens <60-yr, CPF needs lease to youngest owner age 95+.
Rebuild: standard reconstruction S$250â€“450/psf; full new erection S$300â€“700/psf; +10â€“15% fees/contingency; A&A capped 50% of approved GFA.
GCBA: min 1,400 sqm, â‰¤35â€“40% coverage, 2-storey+attic, 3m setbacks, detached only.
Financing 2026: LTV 75%, TDSR 55% stressed at 4%; SORA-linked ~1.55â€“1.80% but stress test requires ~S$15â€“18k/mth gross for S$2.5m.

screening_recommendations.title: Confirm the address is genuinely <1km on the official tool
screening_recommendations.detail: Use OneMap/MOE 1km checker for the exact street number â€” Prince/Princess of Wales, Leedon Park, Belmont, Maryland and Greenmead estates straddle the 1km line; balloting is routine so a borderline address is a real risk.

screening_recommendations.title: Avoid documented flood streets unless elevated/rebuilt high
screening_recommendations.detail: Treat King's Road (Princeâ€“Lutheran), Coronation Walk and the Stevens/Balmoral low point as red flags; request property flood history, check ground elevation, and avoid basements/ground-floor living there.

screening_recommendations.title: Prefer higher-ground or rebuilt-post-2015 homes on regular plots
screening_recommendations.detail: Maryland (sloping) and elevated King's/Coronation sub-roads sit above the eastern Bukit Timah ponding zone; a post-2015 rebuild on a regular (non-corner, rectangular) plot de-risks both flooding and capex.

screening_recommendations.title: Price land + rebuild, not the asking price
screening_recommendations.detail: For an ageing semi-D, budget S$350â€“600/psf GFA rebuild + 10â€“15% fees; a 3,000 sqft rebuild is ~S$1.17â€“2.07m all-in. Discount asking accordingly rather than paying for a tired structure.

screening_recommendations.title: Verify GCBA 2-storey + attic cap before modelling GFA
screening_recommendations.detail: In Leedon Park / Belmont / Cornwall, the 1,400 sqm min, ~35â€“40% coverage and 2-storey+attic envelope hard-cap buildable area â€” confirm via URA before assuming a large rebuild.

screening_recommendations.title: Check conservation gazette on GCB plots
screening_recommendations.detail: Cornwall Gardens and Chatsworth have conserved bungalows (Art-Deco/B&W) where teardown is blocked and subdivision residual must stay â‰¥1,000 sqm â€” this destroys redevelopment optionality, so confirm conservation status.

screening_recommendations.title: Run a full SLA INLIS title search
screening_recommendations.detail: Older estates carry easements, drainage right-of-way, restrictive covenants and possible road-reserve gazettes (acquisition risk); also reconcile surveyed vs title land area before pricing.

screening_recommendations.title: Factor tenure into financing and resale
screening_recommendations.detail: Strongly prefer freehold/999-yr; on 99-yr (incl. Farrer Gardens HDB) expect a S$1â€“1.5m discount, tighter loans <60-yr remaining and CPF limits â€” price the decay.

screening_recommendations.title: Stress-test stamp duty and holding period upfront
screening_recommendations.detail: Citizens pay 20% ABSD on a 2nd property; foreigners 60% plus LDAU 5-yr no-sell/no-rent; SSD bites for 4 years â€” model a â‰¥5-yr hold and full duty before bidding.

screening_recommendations.title: Weigh long-term density catalysts
screening_recommendations.detail: Turf City (15â€“20k homes to ~2050), Holland Plain GLS high-rises and CRL King Albert Park (2032) lift amenity/connectivity but add supply, traffic and competing school demand â€” neutral-to-positive long-term, disruptive near-term.

zoning_planning_notes: Most of the tight 1km ring (King's Rd, Coronation Rd/West, Prince/Princess of Wales) is URA 'Landed Housing' (non-GCBA): 2â€“3 storeys, semi-D/terrace/detached permitted, setbacks vary by road type.
GCBA plots (Leedon Park, Bin Tong, Belmont upper, Cornwall, Chatsworth): detached only, min 1,400 sqm, ~35â€“40% coverage, 2-storey+attic, 3m setbacks; subdivision below 1,400 sqm not allowed.
Conservation overlay on parts of Cornwall Gardens (27 bungalows) and Chatsworth Park limits demolition; subdividing conserved plots requires residual â‰¥1,000 sqm / conserved â‰¥1,800 sqm.
Belmont Road is a blended zone â€” GCBA (premium) plus non-GCBA semi-D â€” so zoning and price tier vary plot by plot.
Low en-bloc/GLS pressure inside the 1km landed core itself; redevelopment intensity is concentrated at the periphery (Turf City, Holland Plain).
future_planning: Bukit Timah Turf City: 15,000â€“20,000 public+private homes phased to ~2050 (first BTO in Bukit Timah in ~40 yrs); 22 heritage buildings retained; 10-min car-lite neighbourhood off Sixth Avenue.
Cross Island Line Phase 2 (~2032): King Albert Park interchange (DT6/CR15), Singapore's deepest station (~50m), boosts connectivity; near-term Blackmore Drive construction disruption.
Holland Plain GLS: Sim Lian awarded (~S$368m, S$1,423 psf ppr; 2nd site ~S$454m, S$1,491 psf ppr 2026), TOP ~2028â€“30; new high-rise + overlapping MGS 1km school demand.
PUB Bukit Timah Canal widening/deepening (Rifle Range Rdâ€“Jalan Kampong Chantek) completing ~2026 to handle ~30% more rainwater; reassess flood risk post-completion.
URA Master Plan 2025 emphasises heritage conservation, car-lite design and green corridors across the broader Bukit Timah district.

data_gaps: Exact 1km boundary per street number not machine-verified here â€” Prince/Princess of Wales, Leedon Park, Belmont, Maryland, Greenmead, Watten House all need OneMap/MOE confirmation.
Granular street-level PUB flood-prone mapping for individual GCBA clusters not extracted â€” consult PUB's flood-prone list directly.
Verified 2024â€“26 transaction caveats per estate are thin; several psf figures are tier estimates rather than confirmed caveats (cross-check URA/EdgeProp/SRX caveats).
Wollaston listed as a lead but not confirmed in sources â€” verify it is a real estate within zone.
Watten Estate ambiguity: some sources place a 'Watten Estate' in D11/Dunearn Rd (condo) vs Watten House condo near Shelford Rd â€” distinguish before relying on it.
best_for: Singapore-citizen / long-term-PR families targeting a genuine <1km Nanyang Primary address who want prime freehold landed and will hold 5+ years and rebuild â€” prioritising verified-inside-1km, higher-ground, regular-plot freehold homes over borderline GCB-prestige plots.
